name: live_method_suite_v1
description: Frozen public structured-judgment cases for live OpenRouter method comparisons. The suite is small enough to rerun, but intentionally spans project prioritization, method design, model routing, onboarding, benchmark rigor, and failure triage.

cases.name: public_artifact_work
cases.description: Prioritize work that makes cardinal-harness more shareable as a serious public artifact.

items.text: Build a preserved real-LLM head-to-head suite comparing scalar/list, ordinal pairwise, and cardinal pairwise judgments on identical attribute-weighted tasks, with request/response/trace/cost artifacts.
Make every checked-in receipt portable from a fresh clone: relative paths, model IDs, prompt hashes, cache hit counts, provider cost semantics, and explicit non-convergence flags.
Remove or quarantine transitive cargo-audit warnings for paste, anyhow, and rand so the public release has a cleaner security posture.
Compress the first-time user path into one obvious command, one tiny request file, one expected output, and one explanation of when cardinal ranking is worth its cost.
Create a larger frozen benchmark suite with multiple task families, repeated runs, equalized budgets, raw artifacts, and a clear statistical report.

attributes.prompt: how much completing this work increases concrete falsifiable evidence for robust LLM list judgment
how much this work improves confidence that a serious external reader would share the artifact
how tractable this work is to implement cleanly without creating a sprawling subsystem

cases.name: judgment_method_properties
cases.description: Rank judging regimes for sorting a list robustly by multiple attributes under real LLM noise.

items.text: Ask the model to assign each item an independent 1-10 or 0-100 score for every attribute, then sort by the weighted sum.
Ask the model to directly return one ranked list after considering all attributes and weights at once.
Ask pairwise questions that only choose which item is better on an attribute, then aggregate wins into a ranking.
Ask pairwise questions for both the better item and how many times more it has the attribute, then fit a latent score model.
Use cardinal pairwise questions plus an uncertainty-aware policy that spends comparisons where top-k uncertainty is highest.

attributes.prompt: resistance to model calibration drift, anchoring, presentation order, and local inconsistency
amount of ranking information extracted per provider call under the same model and prompt budget
ability for another engineer to inspect individual judgments and understand why the final order changed

cases.name: model_policy_options
cases.description: Rank live OpenRouter judging policies for public structured-judgment receipts.

items.text: Fixed anthropic/claude-opus-4.6 policy. High-cost path for public receipts where careful comparative reasoning matters more than spend.
Use anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6 for a frozen reference or disputed cases, then use a cheaper current model for repeated candidate-regime sweeps.
Fixed deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash policy for cheap high-volume smoke tests, prompt iteration, and broad first-pass sweeps.
Use z-ai/glm-5.2 when logprob support, low prompt cost, and large context matter more than maximum brand credibility.
Use moonshotai/kimi-k2-thinking as a diverse reasoning judge for contested comparisons or cross-model adjudication.

attributes.prompt: credibility as a judge model for evidence that will be shown to serious external readers
ability to support many repeated live benchmark runs without wasting provider spend
current OpenRouter availability, parameter support, and fit for structured judgment prompts

cases.name: first_user_path
cases.description: Rank onboarding improvements for an engineer trying cardinal-harness from a fresh clone.

items.text: A five-minute quickstart with one request JSON, one command, one expected top-k output, and one short explanation of the receipt fields.
A guided walkthrough from messy shortlist to attributes, pairwise prompts, cache reuse, report reading, and decision caveats.
A prominent section showing cases where scalar metrics, deterministic sorting, or whole-list ranking are cheaper and more appropriate.
A minimal library example that constructs a MultiRerankRequest, installs a model policy, runs one attribute, and emits a report.
A troubleshooting guide for cache misses, model refusals, cost estimates, non-convergence, and inconsistent pairwise judgments.

attributes.prompt: how much this reduces the time before a new user gets a meaningful successful run
how strongly this prevents users from applying cardinal ranking where it is the wrong tool
how cheap this is to maintain as the code and models evolve

cases.name: benchmark_design_rigor
cases.description: Prioritize benchmark-design requirements for making a live method-comparison result publishable.

items.text: Run every method under a fixed provider-dollar cap and report how much agreement each method buys before the cap is exhausted.
Repeat the same suite across multiple candidate and reference models so method effects are separated from one model's quirks.
Collect a small human-adjudicated slice that can calibrate whether the live LLM reference itself is plausible.
Tag each disagreement by likely source: prompt ambiguity, reference brittleness, ratio-scale misuse, cost constraint, or genuine method weakness.
Freeze the case list, attributes, weights, models, budget policy, and scoring script before running a public benchmark.

attributes.prompt: how much this increases the validity of any public benchmark claim
how much this lets another researcher reproduce or falsify the result
how feasible this is to execute without excessive provider spend or manual effort

cases.name: public_release_risks
cases.description: Rank the risks that would most undermine sharing cardinal-harness widely as a serious artifact.

items.text: The README or report implies method superiority beyond the checked receipt, making a careful reader distrust the whole project.
A fresh clone cannot verify receipts because paths, cache files, generated artifacts, or model metadata are missing.
Dependency audit warnings distract from the artifact's quality and make users hesitate before installing the crate.
Known cases where cardinal performs badly are buried instead of explained as useful constraints on the method.
The first real run requires too many concepts, flags, and files before the user sees a result worth caring about.

attributes.prompt: how much this would damage trust from a careful external reader
how much fixing this would improve the overall release with one focused change
how likely this risk is to occur during public sharing without additional work
